# RefactorKit v0.6.2 Release Notes

RefactorKit `v0.6.2` is the publication patch for the completed `v0.6.0` feature
scope and delivers the first deep non-Java semantic adapter slice:
explicit-toolchain TypeScript/JavaScript intelligence and managed identifier
rename with exact compiler diagnostics, preview, authorization, WAL, recovery and
rollback. API remains `0.2`; TypeScript capabilities remain experimental and are
not an API `1.0` stability claim.

The immutable `v0.6.0` and `v0.6.1` source tags were not published after Windows
release-build failures; no partial release was created and neither tag was moved.
`v0.6.2` aligns release build/test with the successful fresh-checkout branch CI
path and keeps package/native smoke separate while retaining the accepted product
scope.

## Highlights

- Explicit `typescript-lsp-explicit-v1` discovery with hash-bound Node,
  `typescript-language-server`, `tsserver.js`, `typescript.js` and package
  provenance; no implicit npm, package scripts, project scripts or PATH trust.
- Declarative `typescript-config-declarative-v1` Build Model for JSONC configs,
  local extends, references, include/exclude/files, aliases, JS modes, package
  metadata and source ownership.
- Compiler/LSP-backed symbols, workspace search, definition and references with
  cross-session opaque `lsp-symbol-v1` identity.
- `typescript-compiler-exact-v1` no-emit staged diagnostics over immutable
  source/config overlays with compiler filesystem allowlisting and snapshot
  attestation.
- Managed identifier rename catalogue covering classes, interfaces, enums,
  functions/methods, fields/properties, variables/constants, aliases, parameters,
  type parameters, namespaces and internal identifier modules.
- Exact prepare-rename, UTF-16 validation, path/version/ownership checks,
  dynamic-reference and external-consumer refusal/override boundaries.
- CLI, daemon and MCP semantic routing with preview-by-default behavior. Standard
  TypeScript/JavaScript LSP ownership remains with the editor's native client.
- Packaged real-toolchain acceptance for path aliases, re-exports, apply, WAL,
  rollback, real language-server kill/restart and a 111-file kill at durable
  `APPLYING` followed by exact startup recovery.
- Stable capability schema now reports compiler diagnostics separately from
  language-server rename proposals.

## Qualified toolchain

Managed support is qualified for:

- Node.js `22.18.0`;
- `typescript-language-server` `5.1.3`;
- TypeScript `5.9.3`.

The toolchain is external and not bundled in runtime assets. CI installs the
lockfile with package scripts disabled. See
[`docs/releases/v0.6.0-support-matrix.md`](../../docs/releases/v0.6.0-support-matrix.md).

## Compatibility and migration

See [`docs/releases/v0.6.0-migration.md`](../../docs/releases/v0.6.0-migration.md).
Existing Java beta-contract workflows remain compatible. TypeScript semantic IDs
are opaque and must be obtained through search rather than parsed. Managed writes
remain available only through preview/diagnostics/authorization/PatchEngine
surfaces.

## Artifacts

The release workflow replaces the placeholders below in the published body.

- Source tag: _TBD before release_
- Release commit: _TBD before release_
- Runtime assets: `refactorkit-runtime-0.6.2-<platform>.zip`
- Per-platform checksum: `*.zip.sha256`
- Per-platform SPDX JSON SBOM: `refactorkit-runtime-<platform>.spdx.json`
- Attestations: GitHub artifact build provenance and SBOM attestations
- macOS Developer ID/notarization: unsigned (`TD-DIST-001`)
- Windows Authenticode: unsigned (`TD-DIST-001`)

## Acceptance

- [x] full build, 548 Kotlin/JVM tests and 23/23 golden cases;
- [x] native packaged runtime acceptance on four targets;
- [x] real TypeScript toolchain reads and stable IDs;
- [x] exact compiler before/staged/apply diagnostics;
- [x] path-alias and re-export managed rename;
- [x] explicit apply, WAL and exact rollback;
- [x] semantic process kill and provenance-preserving restart;
- [x] daemon kill at durable `APPLYING` and startup compensation;
- [x] hostile metadata/path/stream/frame/process-tree limits;
- [ ] tag workflow, final checksums, SBOM/attestations and independent downloaded
  asset verification (completed by publication).

## Known limitations

- API and TypeScript semantic capabilities remain pre-1.0 experimental surfaces.
- Dynamic/mixed JavaScript remains proposal-only; checked JavaScript requires the
  exact compiler gate.
- Package-based config `extends` is refused without a hash-bound package trust
  policy.
- Advanced move/file rename, organize imports, change signature, extract/inline
  and migration recipes are deferred to `v0.7.x`.
- The 512 MiB limit is V8 old-space, not an OS RSS sandbox.
- External semantic processes are controlled providers, not filesystem/network
  sandboxes.
- macOS and Windows archives are unsigned and may trigger platform publisher
  warnings; checksums and attestations do not replace code signing.

## Verification

```bash
sha256sum -c refactorkit-runtime-0.6.2-<platform>.zip.sha256
python3 scripts/verify-runtime-archive.py \
  refactorkit-runtime-0.6.2-<platform>.zip \
  refactorkit-runtime-0.6.2-<platform>.zip.sha256 \
  --platform <platform>
```

Published tags are immutable. A release defect receives a new patch version.
